hypothesis: If a conditional diffusion model operating in the CIAE latent space and conditioned on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed element lists is used to sample shell-wise catalytic surface compositions, then the generated samples will achieve lower Fréchet Distance to real configurations, lower leakage, and higher cosine similarity to ground-truth compositions than a chemically-agnostic baseline, because conditioning provides chemical specificity that guides sampling toward physically plausible, constraint-compliant configurations.
